task_completion.description: The base project countries_base.gvsproj is loaded in gvSIG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG application is open and the window title or project manager indicates 'countries_base' is loaded.

task_completion.description: The Populated Places layer is added to the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'ne_110m_populated_places' is visible in the Table of Contents (TOC) on the left side of the map view.

task_completion.description: Scale-dependent visibility is configured for the Populated Places layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer Properties dialog for 'ne_110m_populated_places' is open, and a scale dependency setting is enabled with a value of 20000000 (or 1:20,000,000) in the relevant scale field.

task_completion.description: The project is saved as scale_visibility.gvsproj.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved as 'scale_visibility.gvsproj' in the correct directory, or the gvSIG window title updates to 'scale_visibility'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the gvSIG graphical user interface to configure the layer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the gvSIG application being used to configure settings, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The layer data is genuinely loaded into the map.
integrity.visual_evidence: Points representing populated places are visible on the map view when the layer is checked and within the visible scale range.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing the .gvsproj XML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots of text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) open with the .gvsproj file being manually modified.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires saving the project to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/projects/scale_visibility.gvsproj'. The layer name is 'ne_110m_populated_places'. The target scale denominator is 20000000 (for 1:20,000,000). The XML tags <minScale>, <maxScale>, and <scale> are used internally by gvSIG, but the agent should configure this via the gvSIG GUI Layer Properties.
